tasks.title: Infrastructure Prep: Subdomain & SSL Generation
tasks.description: Create the social.samixism.com subdomain in cPanel and generate Let's Encrypt SSL certificates using Certbot.
tasks.details: 1. Log into cPanel and create an 'A' record for 'social.samixism.com' pointing to 49.13.129.43. 2. SSH into the VPS and run `certbot certonly --nginx -d social.samixism.com`. 3. Verify the certificates are successfully generated at `/etc/letsencrypt/live/social.samixism.com/`. Note: Ensure cPanel's AutoSSL does not conflict with Certbot by disabling it for this specific subdomain if necessary.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `certbot certificates` to verify the new domain is listed and valid. Perform a DNS lookup to ensure the subdomain resolves to the correct IP.

tasks.title: Server Resource Tuning & Swap Configuration
tasks.description: Configure server resources to handle the RAM-heavy Temporal and Elasticsearch containers without triggering OOM kills.
tasks.details: Given the 8GB RAM constraint and the heavy footprint of Temporal + ES, create a 4GB swap file to prevent Out-Of-Memory (OOM) crashes. 1. Run `fallocate -l 4G /swapfile`, `chmod 600 /swapfile`, `mkswap /swapfile`, and `swapon /swapfile`. 2. Add `/swapfile swap swap defaults 0 0` to `/etc/fstab`. 3. Adjust swappiness: `sysctl vm.swappiness=10` and persist in `/etc/sysctl.conf`. 4. Prepare Elasticsearch heap limits for the Docker compose file (`ES_JAVA_OPTS="-Xms256m -Xmx256m"`).
tasks.testStrategy: Run `free -h` to verify the 4GB swap space is active. Run `cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness` to verify it is set to 10.

tasks.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy Configuration
tasks.description: Configure Engintron/Nginx to route traffic to the Postiz Docker container while adhering to strict server rules.
tasks.details: Edit `/etc/nginx/conf.d/custom_rules.conf` (NEVER default.conf). Add a server block for `social.samixism.com` listening on 443 with SSL certs from Task 144. Proxy pass to `http://127.0.0.1:4007`. Crucially, include `proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;`, `proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-SSL on;`, and WebSocket support (`Upgrade $http_upgrade`, `Connection 'upgrade'`). To allow iframe embedding in Phase 2, add `add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self' https://client.samixism.com";` and remove any restrictive `X-Frame-Options` headers.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `nginx -t` to validate the configuration syntax. If successful, run `systemctl reload nginx`. Verify with `curl -I https://social.samixism.com` (should return 502 Bad Gateway until Docker is up, but SSL should be valid).

tasks.title: Postiz Docker Compose Architecture Setup
tasks.description: Clone and configure the Postiz Docker Compose stack, resolving port conflicts and setting resource limits.
tasks.details: 1. Create directory `/home/clientdash/postiz/`. 2. Create `docker-compose.yml` based on the official Postiz AGPL-3.0 repo. 3. Map the main Postiz app port to `4007:5000`. 4. Remap Temporal UI port from `8080:8080` to `8081:8080` to avoid Apache conflicts. 5. Add memory limits to the `temporal-elasticsearch` service (`mem_limit: 512m`) and pass `ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms256m -Xmx256m`. 6. Ensure all services use a dedicated custom bridge network to avoid cPanel iptables conflicts.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose config` to validate the YAML file. Ensure no port 8080 bindings exist in the output.

tasks.title: Environment Variables & Secrets Management
tasks.description: Configure the `.env` file for Postiz, Temporal, and Database connections.
tasks.details: Create `/home/clientdash/postiz/.env`. Generate a secure JWT secret using `openssl rand -base64 64`. Set `MAIN_URL=https://social.samixism.com`, `FRONTEND_URL=https://social.samixism.com`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://social.samixism.com/api`. Configure `DATABASE_URL` and `REDIS_URL` to point to the internal Docker service names. Set `IS_GENERAL=true`, `DISABLE_REGISTRATION=true` (to restrict access to admins), and `STORAGE_PROVIDER=local` with `UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads` mapped to a persistent Docker volume.
tasks.testStrategy: Review the `.env` file manually to ensure no trailing spaces, correct URL schemes (https), and strong generated passwords for Postgres and JWT.

tasks.title: Deploy and Initialize Postiz Stack
tasks.description: Start the Docker Compose stack, verify container health, and initialize the database.
tasks.details: Run `docker compose up -d` in the Postiz directory. Temporal and Elasticsearch take time to initialize; the Postiz backend might crash and restart initially. Configure `restart: unless-stopped` in the compose file to handle this. Monitor the logs of the `postiz-app` container to ensure Prisma migrations run successfully and the NestJS backend connects to Temporal (`temporal:7233`) and Postgres.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose ps` and ensure all 8 services are 'Up' and 'healthy'. Check `docker compose logs postiz-app` for 'Nest application successfully started'. Visit `https://social.samixism.com` to see the login screen.

tasks.title: Temporal UI Security & Access Configuration
tasks.description: Secure the Temporal UI exposed on port 8081 to prevent unauthorized access to workflow data.
tasks.details: Since Temporal UI is exposed on `http://49.13.129.43:8081` and lacks built-in auth, it must be secured. Option A: Bind the port only to localhost (`127.0.0.1:8081:8080`) and use an SSH tunnel to access it. Option B: Create another Nginx server block (e.g., `temporal.samixism.com`) proxying to 8081 with `auth_basic` enabled. Implement Option A for immediate security, documenting the SSH tunnel command (`ssh -L 8081:localhost:8081 user@49.13.129.43`) in the SERVER_PLAYBOOK.md.
tasks.testStrategy: Attempt to access `http://49.13.129.43:8081` from a public IP; it should fail or be rejected. Establish an SSH tunnel and access `http://localhost:8081` locally to verify Temporal UI loads.

tasks.title: Client Dashboard Frontend Integration
tasks.description: Embed Postiz into the existing Client Dashboard UI via an iframe and sidebar navigation.
tasks.details: In the Client Dashboard React/Next.js codebase: 1. Add a new navigation item in the Sidebar component labeled 'Social Media' using the Lucide `Share2` icon. 2. Create a new route/page at `/social`. 3. Implement an iframe: `<iframe src="https://social.samixism.com" className="w-full h-[calc(100vh-64px)] border-none rounded-md" title="Social Media Scheduler" allow="clipboard-write; clipboard-read" />`. Ensure the dashboard's layout accommodates the full width/height.
tasks.testStrategy: Log into the Client Dashboard, click 'Social Media' in the sidebar. Verify the Postiz login page renders inside the iframe without CORS or X-Frame-Options errors. Test responsive behavior on mobile views.

tasks.title: Social Media Platform API Setup (MVP Platforms)
tasks.description: Register developer applications for core social platforms (X/Twitter, LinkedIn) and configure Postiz.
tasks.details: To allow users to schedule posts, OAuth apps must be created. 1. X/Twitter: Create an app in the Twitter Developer Portal, set callback URL to `https://social.samixism.com/api/auth/callback/twitter`, obtain API Key/Secret. 2. LinkedIn: Create an app in LinkedIn Developer Portal, request 'Share on LinkedIn' and 'Sign In with LinkedIn' products, set callback, obtain Client ID/Secret. 3. Add these credentials to the Postiz `.env` file (`X_API_KEY`, `LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID`, etc.) and restart the Postiz container.
tasks.testStrategy: Log into Postiz as an admin. Navigate to 'Connections' or 'Providers'. Attempt to connect a test X (Twitter) account and a LinkedIn account. Verify the OAuth flow completes successfully and the accounts appear as connected.

tasks.title: System Monitoring & Automated Backups
tasks.description: Implement resource monitoring and database backup scripts to ensure stability and data safety.
tasks.details: 1. Create a bash script `/home/clientdash/scripts/backup_postiz.sh` that runs `docker exec postiz-postgres pg_dump -U postiz-user postiz-db-local > /home/clientdash/backups/postiz_$(date +%F).sql`. 2. Add a cron job to run this daily at 3 AM (`0 3 * * *`). 3. Update the existing `system-health-monitor.sh` (if present) or create a simple cron to check RAM usage (`free -m`) and log warnings if available RAM drops below 10% to proactively catch Temporal/ES memory leaks.
tasks.testStrategy: Execute the backup script manually and verify the `.sql` file is created and contains valid PostgreSQL dump data. Check `crontab -l` to ensure the job is scheduled.
